entry.title: A Comprehensive Guide to Electronic Invoicing enablement in SAP Business ByDesign
entry.content:
Electronic invoicing has emerged as one of the foremost legal requirements imposed by governments worldwide in recent years. It is anticipated to become the prevailing method for global business-to-business (B2B) invoice exchange in the imminent future.

Given the multitude of governments instituting this legal mandate, it is crucial for customers with a global presence to understand the potential strategies for enabling electronic invoicing in SAP Business ByDesign.

This article aims to consolidate all pertinent information in one location, serving as a general guide on the approach to be taken for electronic invoicing in SAP Business ByDesign for any given country.

INDIA

Electronic Invoicing in India commenced on October 1, 2020, with a phased implementation approach based on the aggregated turnover of businesses.

Correlated references:

India Electronic Invoicing – Help Documentation

India E-Invoice approaches in SAP Business ByDesign - Blog

Mass Update of Legal Approval Data – Help Documentation, Topic Video

Intracompany Stock Transfer Orders – Help Documentation, Topic Video

Credit Memos and Debit Notes against Stock Transfer Order – Help Documentation, Topic Video

Commercial Credit and Debit Notes for India – Help Documentation, Topic Video

QR Code on B2C Invoices - Help Documentation, Topic Video

 

CHINA

Effective December 1, 2024, the China Tax Bureau announced the nationwide adoption and availability of Fully Digitalized Electronic Invoices (FDEI) for all taxpayers.

Correlated references:

Fully Digitalized Electronic Invoice – Help Documentation, Topic Video

Detailed information pertaining to the enablement of the older regulation for the Golden Tax system in China within SAP Business ByDesign can be accessed here.

 

MEXICO

Invoice documents issued by Mexican companies must adhere to the legal requirements specified by the Servicio de Administración Tributaria (SAT), the Mexican tax authority.

Correlated references:

Help Documentation

 

JAPAN

In Japan, companies are obligated to carry out the 'Shimeseikyu' process, whereby a company requests its customers to settle multiple accounts receivable items (Customer Invoices) simultaneously. SAP Business ByDesign facilitates this process through Invoice Summary Documents.

Correlated references - Help Documentation, Blog

Electronic Invoicing in Japan commenced on October 1, 2023. This capability is supported through the integration of SAP Business ByDesign with the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition. The recommended payload specification for Japan is PINT.

Correlated references:

Help Documentation

Topic Video

 

SOUTH KOREA

In South Korea, electronic tax invoices can be generated for all transactions involving output tax. These invoices are required to be submitted to the National Tax Service (NTS), with the approval number maintained in the system. SAP Business ByDesign supports this through its Electronic Tax Invoice feature.

Correlated references:

Help Documentation

 

ISRAEL

The Israeli Tax Authority has mandated electronic Invoicing, effective from May 5, 2024. This has been enabled in SAP Business ByDesign with the addition of the Allocation Number field in relevant business scenarios and can be further automated with the assistance of implementation partners.

Correlated references:

Help Documentation

Topic Video

Sequential Document Numbering for Self-Invoice

Sequential Document Numbering for ERS Credit Memo

 

AUSTRALIA / NEW ZEALAND

The Australian and New Zealand Peppol Authorities have introduced the new PINT (Peppol International Invoice) format, mandated for use starting in May 2025. This is facilitated through the integration of SAP Business ByDesign with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.

Correlated references:

Help Documentation

 

GERMANY

The German government has introduced mandatory electronic invoicing for domestic B2B transactions, effective from January 1, 2025. SAP Business ByDesign already supports the exchange of electronic customer and supplier invoices.

Correlated references:

Receiving E-Invoices with SAP Business ByDesign - Blog

German xRechnung Templates for SAP Business ByDesign - Blog

 

SUPPORTED COUNTRIES ON PEPPOL NETWORK

It is a legal requirement in numerous countries that business transaction documents are electronically exchanged among business partners, legal authorities, or other public entities (business-to-government, B2G) or between your company and your business partners (business-to-business, B2B). Several countries have adopted the Peppol eDelivery network as the standard for electronic transfer of documents. SAP Business ByDesign enables this information exchange through integration with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.

With this integration, please find below

Supported Business Scenarios

Supported Document Types for given countries/regions

Correlated references:

Blog: Integrating SAP Business ByDesign to Peppol Network

 

ENABLEMENT FOR NON-LOCALIZED COUNTRIES

In addition to the 21 localized countries supported by SAP Business ByDesign, electronic invoicing support for non-localized countries is enabled via a combination of standard enablement and the Localization Toolkit. Detailed information on standard enablement can be found on the ‘Country/Region-Specific Features’ page of the SAP Help Portal. Examples for Greece and Ukraine are available therein.

Moreover, using the localization toolkit guidelines for SAP Business ByDesign, businesses can develop add-ons and maintain business configurations to meet country/region-specific requirements.

Correlated references:

Localization Toolkit for SAP Business ByDesign

This comprehensive course covers essential topics such as taxes, tax calculation, tax reporting, electronic submission of tax returns, generation of custom electronic files, and language translation tools. The 'Week 3' section focuses on learning content specific to custom electronic file generation, including electronic invoices.

SAP Community for SAP Business ByDesign Localization

Blog for Electronic Invoicing using Web Services

Blog for Electronic Invoicing using XSLT Repository

 

Updates regarding upcoming regulations will be published in the regulatory change manager. Please follow these updates to stay informed about the status and developments related to regulatory changes.

entry.title: Update a translated simulation on Producer without touching the old translated bubbles
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I recently updated an English simulation by adding new steps and bubbles. This simulation had previously been machine translated into six languages. To ensure that the changes in the English simulation are reflected in the translated versions, I performed machine translation again. While the updates were successfully applied to the other translated simulations, the previously translated and reviewed bubbles were overwritten with new translations.

Is there a way to select specific bubbles to exclude from the machine translation process, so that the old translations, which have already been reviewed by our language experts, are retained?

Thank you for your guidance.

entry.title: Re: Error FAA_POST214 when reversing Asset Document of Le...
entry.content:
Hi Jeremy, Thanks for your input.

I have posted depreciation for all 12 periods of 2024 and then successfully closed the 2024 through t-code FAA_CMP. Now only one year (2025) is open to meet the requirement of error FAA_POST214. I also created new segment in FAA_CMP with Sequence no.2 and LDT date as 31.12.2024. (Previous segment is no.1 which has LDT date of 31.12.2023 and its status is "Completed").

Now as I execute AB08, this error is gone but another error is coming "Contact your system administrator (table error) Message no. AA422".

For this, I followed Note 3555206 and updated Table ANLA with Sequence no.2 and LDT date as 31.12.2024. Still AB08 is giving same error AA422. FYI, i also checked table ANLC and it contains data for 2023, 2024 and 2025.

entry.title: Negative Balances in AR Aging Report Due to FBRA on Partial Clearings (SAP S/4HANA)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team, 

We’re facing an ongoing issue in our custom AR Aging report. After running FBRA (on 12.02.2025) to reverse incorrect partial clearings from 2022 for Customer 100009, the Aging report now shows negative balances in retro reports (2022 and 2023).

These DZ (clearing reversal) documents are technically correct per FI postings, but the CFO expects no negatives in the Aging Report. If we exclude these DZ docs, the report doesn’t match FS or FBL3N.

Goal: Build aging logic that matches Financial Statements and FBL3N, both retrospectively and prospectively, but avoids showing negative AR balances due to FBRA.

Any advice on handling FBRA and DZ documents in custom aging reports? Can we simulate prior status or apply dynamic logic for cleared history?

entry.title: Re: Performance comparison: FILTER vs LOOP vs FOR
entry.content: You are not comparing the same thing between "VALUE #( FOR item" and "LOOP AT ASSIGNING <item>". You should compare with field symbols in both (i.e. "VALUE #( FOR <item>").
